world.title: Afghans Who Helped U.S. Forces Say They’re Being Pushed Back to the Taliban
world.description: Once promised a move to the United States, Afghan refugees who helped U.S. forces say they face ‘bad or worse’ options: resettlement to Congo or returning home to live under the Taliban.

world.title: Police detain Modiin man for wearing kippa with Palestinian flag, cut it out
world.description: 'Now it was being banned from my head, my religious identity itself,' Alex Sinclair says, filing complaint against officers who falsely claim displaying Palestinian flag is illegal

world.title: Ghana summons South African high envoy over xenophobic attacks
world.description: Ghana‘s ministry of foreign affairs says it has summoned Thando Dalamba, South African acting high commissioner in Ghana, over recent xenophobic attacks and anti-foreigner protests in South Africa.

world.title: Caritas renews its commitment to safe water and healthcare
world.description: Caritas Internationalis brought a strong grassroots voice to one of the most urgent challenges in global health this week, as more than 100 faith leaders, health experts and practitioners convened in Rome for a landmark two-day conference on water, sanitation and hygiene in healthcare facilities. Held on 22–23 April under the patronage of the Dicastery […]

world.title: Truth is not enough - journalism now needs more than facts
world.description: Notes from a roundtable on information integrity, and why journalists need to stop assuming people care about facts

world.title: US Department of Justice watchdog announces Epstein files audit
world.description: The audit follows months of controversy over censorship, missed deadlines and the publication of survivors' details.

world.title: Global conflicts see increase of health care worker harm
world.description: Attacks on health care in conflict zones are occurring at unprecedented levels, according to Safeguarding Health Care in Conflict Coalition and Médecins Sans Frontières (Doctors Without Borders). Rice’s Baker Institute for Public Policy on April X hosted global leaders in the field and frontline medical professionals to discuss the erosion of protections for health care in conflict zones and explore solutions to ensure these critical rules are respected.

world.title: One dead after shooting at US mall, bystanders hit as rival groups clash
world.description: Authorities said the shooting on Thursday at the Mall of Louisiana in Baton Rouge city stemmed from a clash between two groups and was not a random attack.

business.title: US bar group scraps questions on mental health from lawyer fitness review
business.description: Aspiring U.S. lawyers should not be required to disclose details of their mental health conditions or treatment in order to become licensed to practice, a key organization has concluded.

business.title: Meta to cut 10% of staff as it pours billions into AI
business.description: Meta said on Thursday it plans to lay off roughly 10% of its workforce, or about 8,000 people, the latest in a string of tech industry layoffs fueled in part by artificial intelligence.

business.title: Cadence CEO on the AI boom and what it means for humans
business.description: Cadence's Anirudh Devgan talked to Fortune about how technology will impact the future.

business.title: NTSB report describes chaos at LaGuardia Airport ahead of deadly Air Canada crash
business.description: The March 22 crash came as seven different emergency vehicles scrambled to respond to another plane at the airport.

business.title: This sold-out Toronto sewing class is full of people learning to mend their clothes

business.title: Home of the Year awards: WA builders pick up prizes in esteemed national recognition
business.description: Two West Australian builders were recognised in this year’s Australian Home of the Year awards, which were announced last weekend.

business.title: Cyber Risk in Asia Moves from Technical Threat to Enterprise Liability and Insurance Imperative
business.description: Cyber risk has become a defining enterprise challenge for companies operating in Asia. A recent regional risk survey underscores what many boards and risk leaders already recognize: cyber attacks and data breaches remain at the top of business risk rankings, alongside supply chain disruption, natural disasters, and geopolitical volatility.

science.title: NOAA Golden Orb Identification: Scientists Solve Deep-Sea Mystery After Years of Study
science.description: Scientists have identified the mysterious “golden orb” found deep in the Gulf of Alaska as part of a rare deep-sea anemone, Relicanthus daphneae. science.title: These Parasitic Caterpillars Trick Ants and Get Treated Like Queens
science.description: Cunning caterpillars have mastered the art of timing, using it to quietly blend into ant colonies and live like queens.

science.title: Seismic ‘whiplash’ - new research shows what happens when earthquakes stop suddenly
science.description: By analysing seismic recordings taken within a few kilometres of faults, a ground motion signal we call a "stopping phase" has been identified.

science.title: Scientists discover how the Twelve Apostles were formed - and their real age
science.description: Scientists at the University of Melbourne have uncovered for the first time how Australia’s iconic Twelve Apostles were formed, finding tectonic plate movements over millions of years lifted and tilted the giant structures out of the sea.
